name: Morning at the Museum
venue: Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History
description: Early-access, sensory-safe experience at Smithsonian museums before public opening. Reduced crowds, dimmed lights, quiet spaces, and sensory bags available. Free admission.

name: Saturday Sensations Sensory Room
description: Drop-in sensory room experience with specialized equipment and calming environments for children and families with sensory processing needs.

name: AMC Sensory Friendly Films
description: Sensory-friendly movie screenings with lights turned up, sound turned down, and a judgment-free environment where kids can move, talk, and be themselves.

name: Regal Sensory Friendly Screenings
description: Sensory-friendly movie showings with adjusted lighting and sound levels for a more comfortable cinema experience for individuals with sensory sensitivities.

name: Sensory-Friendly Performances at the Kennedy Center
venue: The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts
description: Modified theater performances with adjusted sound and lighting, relaxed house rules, quiet areas, and pre-visit social stories to help attendees prepare.

name: Sensory-Friendly Shopping Hours at Chuck E. Cheese
description: Sensory-sensitive Sundays and Tuesdays with dimmed lights, reduced sounds, limited menu options, and a calmer environment for children with autism and sensory needs.

name: Autism Swim Program
description: Specialized swim lessons designed for individuals with autism, using structured teaching methods, visual supports, and patient 1-on-1 instruction in a sensory-considerate pool environment.

name: Fairfax County Adaptive Recreation Programs
description: Adaptive recreation programs including sports, arts, social clubs, and camp programs for youth and adults with disabilities through Fairfax County Neighborhood and Community Services.

name: Smithsonian Access Programs
description: Multiple accessibility programs across Smithsonian museums including sign language tours, tactile experiences, verbal description tours, and sensory-friendly events.

name: Special Olympics Virginia Events
description: Year-round sports training and competitions for individuals with intellectual disabilities including track and field, basketball, swimming, bowling, bocce, and more.

name: Sensory Storytime
description: Adapted storytime sessions for children with sensory processing differences, autism, and other developmental disabilities. Features visual schedules, fidgets, movement breaks, and a calm environment.

name: Autism Speaks Walk
description: Annual community walk events to raise funds and awareness for autism research and support services. Family-friendly events with activities, resource fairs, and entertainment.

name: Buddy Ball Baseball
description: Adapted baseball program pairing children with disabilities with volunteer buddies. Players of all ability levels can participate in a supportive, fun team environment.
